title: Samantha's Flight to Serbia
author: Dady Fredy
subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
dedication: For Samantha — born in Angeles City, roots in Belgrade.

pages.title: Samantha's Flight to Serbia
pages.subtitle: A Bedtime Story by Dady Fredy
pages.dedication: For Samantha — born in Angeles City, roots in Belgrade.

lines: The suitcase was packed and the passports were found,
And Mommy said softly, "We're leaving the ground."
Dady Fredy knelt down with a wink and a smile,
"We're flying to Serbia, anak — in style."
The airplane climbed high through the dark, starry sky,
And Samantha pressed close as the world floated by.
The houses grew tiny, the forests and seas,
While the ocean below shimmered gold in the breeze.
"Where are we going?" she whispered with wonder,
With moonlight above and the city lights under.
The rivers like silver, the highways aflame—
And Mommy said softly, "Where Dady began."
They landed in Belgrade when morning was new,
Where the Danube flowed wide under pale morning blue.
The cobblestones whispered beneath little feet,
As if the old city had risen to meet.
They walked hand in hand through Kalemegdan's walls,
Where fortress towers stood ancient and tall.
Samantha ran laughing through archways of stone,
And the wind seemed to murmur, "Welcome back home."
They sat by the river and shared warm burek,
Flaky and golden on the warm stone deck.
Dady said, "Taste it — this city runs through you,"
As the bells of the St. Sava rang soft and rang true.
They wandered through Skadarlija, music drifting along,
Where lanterns glowed amber and old fiddles sang on.
An old man was humming a tune low and so sweet,
And Samantha swayed gently, half-asleep on her feet.
The streetlamps of Belgrade blinked on one by one,
As the city grew quiet, the long day now done.
They carried Samantha through warm evening air,
Past gardens and churches and stairways of prayer.
Dady Fredy spread blankets beneath the night sky,
Where the stars over Serbia drifted on by.
And Mommy curled close, and they held her between—
The safest, most beautiful place she had been.
"Dady was born here," he said, looking round,
"And Mommy grew up on warm island ground.
They met in Angeles — and right from the start,
You were the song living inside every heart."
"Two countries, two stories, now woven in one—
The Philippines, Serbia… and you, little sun.
Wherever we travel, wherever we roam,
With us, my child… you are always at home."
"So close your eyes now, Samantha, my dear…
Mommy and Dady are always right here.
Dream of the river, the fortress, the song—
You come from a love that is two countries strong."
Goodnight, Samantha.
Goodnight, anak.
